title: Lauren J. Joseph’s New Novel Turns the Trans Rock Muse Into an Erotic Phantasmagoria
description:
“I’d like to send this one out to Lou and Rachel,” Lou Reed croons at the end of the title track to his 1975 album Coney Island Baby. At the time, Reed was head over heels with Rachel Humphries, a Mexican-American trans woman who appeared alongside him on album covers, on stage, and through his […]

The post Lauren J. Joseph’s New Novel Turns the Trans Rock
hook: Lou Reed's trans muse becomes the fever dream at the center of a debut novel that rewrites rock mythology from the inside.

title: Bitch: a history
description:
The word can morph from noun to verb to adjective, from dog to human, from female to male. What will it do next?

- by Karen Stollznow
hook: How a single word became a weapon, a reclamation, and a mirror for women's rage across centuries of language battles.

title: undertone, 2026’s Scariest Movie So Far, Conjures Filmmaker Ian Tuason’s Real-life Demons
description: “I was never really into the occult before making this movie,” says Ian Tuason, writer-director of the new A24 horror film undertone. “After doing research, I started g
hook: A filmmaker transforms his own psychological breakdown into something genuinely unsettling—horror as exorcism, not spectacle.

title: Kafka’s Misdiagnosis
description: “One of the lessons of Kafkian neurosis is that human beings are the architects of their own cages—yet the very ingenuity by which we entrap ourselves points to a freedom that remains untamed.”
hook: Kafka's neurosis reveals something darker: we build our own cages with such precision it proves we're somehow still free.

title: La Vida Loca
description: Even as the stories gets dark, Jaime Hernandez’s linework maintains a tangible lust for life.
hook: Even when Jaime Hernandez's stories turn brutal, his lines dance—proof that style can hold joy while depicting devastation.

title: Jet Propulsion Laboratory: Ben Cardew on The Pivotal Role of Stereolab’s ‘Super-Electric’
description: Ben Cardew's new book, Space Age Batchelor Pad Music: The Story Of Stereolab In 20 Songs, recounts the tale of Stereolab in 20 songs that represent certain vital aspects of the band’s make up (from romance, to collage and repetition). In this extract, he looks at the idea of 'propulsion', as it relates to ‘Super-Electric’, the title track of the band’s second EP<
hook: How Stereolab's obsessive, meticulous sound became an architecture for living—the real story behind twenty perfect songs.

title: “You Have to Adjust the Sails to the Winds”: Graham Parkes on Wishful Thinking
description: Love is one of the most powerful forces in the universe. It’s been the subject of some of the best films ever made for good reason, as it’s something that can grab hold of not just your desires, but your very soul as you sear
hook: A director talks love, compromise, and the constant adjustment required to keep two people moving in the same direction.
